{
  "analysis": "Let me continue analyzing the data section and look at the complete rendering loop to understand the exact algorithm.",
  "plan": "Look at the data at 0x485a58 (which contains important vectors), and examine the inner loop of main to understand how each pixel is computed.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 << 'EOF'
import struct

with open('/app/mystery', 'rb') as f:
    # First load segment at offset 0
    f.seek(0)
    elf = f.read(64)
    e_phoff = struct.unpack('<Q', elf[32:40])[0]
    e_phentsize = struct.unpack('<H', elf[54:56])[0]
    e_phnum = struct.unpack('<H', elf[56:58])[0]
    
    # Find the third load segment (vaddr=0x480000)
    f.seek(e_phoff)
    for i in range(e_phnum):
        phdr = f.read(e_phentsize)
        p_type = struct.unpack('<I', phdr[0:4])[0]
        if p_type == 1:
            p_offset = struct.unpack('<Q', phdr[8:16])[0]
            p_vaddr = struct.unpack('<Q', phdr[16:24])[0]
            p_filesz = struct.unpack('<Q', phdr[32:40])[0]
            if p_vaddr <= 0x485a58 < p_vaddr + p_filesz:
                file_off = p_offset + (0x485a58 - p_vaddr)
                print(f"0x485a58 file offset: 0x{file_off:x}")
                f.seek(file_off)
                data = f.read(128)
                for j in range(0, len(data), 4):
                    val = struct.unpack('<f', data[j:j+4])[0]
                    addr = 0x485a58 + j
                    print(f"  0x{addr:06x}: {val:.10f}")
                break
EOF
",
      "duration": 0.3
    }
